summary: Farm-data infrastructure that cleans and structures agribusiness data.
editor_note: The sharp part is treating farm data like payments data: invisible infrastructure with clear downstream value. Leaf connects OEMs, imagery, weather, soil labs, and farm management systems, then standardizes field data for dashboards, reports, AI, insurance, and product models; its funding source says it processes 20% of global crop acres annually. Unlike generic agtech dashboards, it is the plumbing other agribusiness tools build on. The risk is provider integrations and data rights.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes Leaf as a data and intelligence platform for agribusiness marketing, product, R&D, and commercial teams.
Leaf says it connects OEMs, imagery, weather, soil labs, and farm management systems, then standardizes and spatially allocates farm data.
The funding announcement says Leaf processes farm data covering 20% of global crop acres annually.

market_context.primary_user: Agribusiness teams building marketing, insurance, product, and AI workflows on top of farm-level data.
market_context.differentiation: Leaf focuses on the normalization and integration layer that other agribusiness applications need before analysis.
market_context.why_now: Agribusiness AI requires clean, permissioned, cross-provider field data instead of scattered OEM, weather, imagery, and lab feeds.

risks.claim: The open question is whether Leaf can keep data rights, provider integrations, and agribusiness buyer needs aligned at global scale.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
